content: If you're still stuck, this boss has a unique mechanic which you have to kill the front guy before the guy at the back dies otherwise it forces fusion. This makes it so that the boss loses ~70% of it's health as soon as it phases. This makes a single target damage dealer significantly better (you have ghelot who does single target)
ghelot thais horla aigis would work and get you through ch9 too
I just copied the wheel from leaderboards, everyone will have different wheels available.   The guides recommended above are a good choice

Hello, just got into the game and looking for any advice!

I've started playing during arachne banner for a few days. Didn't play for a few months, a bit busy to learn a new game. Recently have some time, then come back a week ago. Here are my character/wheel. 

I'm mainly playing full ultra. Clementine, horla, arachne, pollux. Currently on chapter 6-x (early chapter 6 in story). Just assume ultra as my favorite.

Atm i'm trying to get pollux to e3, planning to use this month (...) to get e1 horla.

I haven't try other faction(?) much beside ultra. If learning to use other faction is it better to try full faction first or not? I prefer to try it with full faction if possible, but if it's not advisable how do i go about it?

Additionally, any advice on which character/character type to get next? How to progress further, focus level/skill/other? If exist, what break point to stop then focus other team?

If i'm planning to play this game casually, is it possible to get most seasonal/weekly rewards? 

Would appreciate any advice beside what is mentioned above. Thank you in advance for the help!

Hey there and welcome back : )

First off : yes playing casually does not prevent you from grabbing activity reward. You basically just need to play 50 cards daily which should take about 15 minutes if you're hurried.

And yes, I would recommand play pure chaos, pure caro and pure æquor in a dozen fight to get a better grip at how they work.

( playing dual realms can come later, when you're looking to set up you 5 D-tide waves )

For additionnal advice on specific awakeners you can try asking in https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1433513650299076780 ( but please do fiddle a bit with them first ).

Honestly, if you manage Clem and Arachne the others should not pose much issue.

Upping your teams' level does matter a lot but doesn't consume materials you need elsewhere.
Pulling for awakener duplicates can change a big deal depending on wvich awakener. You can find details and preview how the grow in their profile.

Your roster is already quite varied but getting a few more limiteds wouldn't hurt ( they are usually more powerful, though not always requiered for progression ).

And then, there's wheels.
You can check Cheri's handbook for advices on which to give to whom.
Wheither you wale and chase +12 wheels ( allowing you to equip dual SSR ) or play free and focus on diversity / awakener enlightenment is your choice.

[AT LEAST 160 FREE STANDARD PULLS]

Go to the thread below for helpful Beginner guides and Redemption codes 

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1509396840330825758/1509396840330825758

Okay, got it. Will continue playing pure faction. Felt easier to learn and manage for first time.

Well, materials gain is dependant on stamina no? I thought it was, so I assume you need to focus or the other. If not, then great.

I try getting a decent roster from standard banner. A bit hesitant to commit for other limited, don't know who to go for. Still not familiar with how much am getting from weekly/seasonal rewards. Atm am overflowing because new player, so has a lot of contents to do. Don't want to overdid aiming for limited.

I did read cheri handbook, felt like read it but didn't stick to my head yet cause unfamiliar.

I'm planning to get caro team up and running for my second team, helot saya aigis faint. Then either chaos(ramona, sr healing lady, and 2 ssr) or aquor (the 4 i have). 

Is it sound or better just focus ultra and caro for now? 

For d-tide, i tried doing it. Managed to clear wave 4 with my ultra. Not confident can clear 3 more wave beside caro team if only level 40. 

If focusing on d-tide, maybe if overlevel to level 50 I can do it (the earliest level). But the rewards felt neglible compared to materials needed for level 50. 2500 lightless currency and 250 silver (?).

On other note, i don't know how steep leveling become the further up you go. Maybe it's better to just built 5 team instead because how steep it is.

Yup, you mostly gain lvl up composants from menophin usage.
You can spend it in the regular content but the event usually provide a more efficient use for them

If you main ultra, I would at least up your Pollux to E3 since you just need 1 more duplicate and it really boosts him.
Saya is really efficient in most teams, Helot:Catena is a pretty regular caro dps and Corposant truly shines in discard/shuffle teams ( i.e. with Saya )
If you got any specific content from Cheri's you want clarification about, you can ask here : )

D-tide resets regularly so yeah, it's an invesment, but the rewards keep coming.

Clearing all 5 waves takes precedence to challenging higher alert levels.

You should be able to easily clear at least 1 more wave by borrowing a strong awakener from someone else ( only possible once across all 5 waves, though )

Up to lvl 40 is affordable. Until 60 is rather expensive but doable for a dozen awakeners given some time.
Above 60 is *very* expensive

I don't recommand focusing on D-Tide for now.
You will naturally strenghten your teams as you go through the story.

Yeah, d-tide felt like something that you naturally do. Not something focusing as hard to do. Maybe if hardcore, learning hard can split teams based on each character strength instead of pure faction bonus. Afraid not possible for me sadly.

Okay, that's good to know. Will prioritize clearing instead of higher level.

Oh true, support can help massively.
( Writing that but I'm still on chapter 4 after 7 weeks because I'm ramping up my teams first ... -.- )
Yeah. Corrosion, weakness and vulnerable can save a game
Aldo, some teams focus solely and poison / counter ( or final verdict 😄 )

Is it level 40 edify 3 or edify 4 the affordable one?

My hesitation to go leveling beside caro and ultra is idk how they fare later on, can they clear it at least or not. If can clear than is okay. Just afraid to invest then ultimately fail, so taking it slow with commiting to leveling. Beside i don't know if i have enough materials atm to level 40 5 teams. 

Prefer getting it(materials) naturally as opposed to focusing now is maybe when i have materials to level 4 teams, there'll be new limited that could be used.
Whaddya mean " eddify" ?
The limit break to extend level range
You can level every one to 40. It's not that expensive and you get some silver too
Oh yeah. Well, both really. The real stopgap are potions and rose scripts there
Is it level 40 then stop, no need to edify to allow next level range or can you do the limit break to allow next level range cause it's still relatively cheap?
Plus, you want 5 teams *at least* lvl 40, which means 19 awakeners ( +1 borrowed from someone else )

~~Every level break yields a core. Worth it I say u.u~~
Scratch that, I'm mixing up with affinity

Do you have a chart or link somewhere which list necessary ingredient to level up character to each level?

Like to level 40, you need x y z by this amount, for skill this amount, etc

Hmmm not sure there is. It's really easy to come by actually.
Just clear the current event store and you'll have an idea of what you can do in a month
Also, starting lvl 40 I think you need another kind of currency which makes you stock up on the lower-rank one weither you want it or not

Honestly, just take it easy and upgrade your 10 favorites. The rest should follow with the leftover

About Arachne : pairing her with caro gives her more opportunities for final verdict : the health loss bypasses shield so you need some actual heal.
Aigis and Faint keep you going in that way

If you have any other tips/advice, would love to hear it.

Beside that, I don't have anything else to ask atm. Maybe if something come up, will ask. Thank you for the help. Really appreciated it! <:Horla_shy:1468259514091573350>

You needn't hesitate to ping me, here are in https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1433513650299076780

Chaos 24? Hmm... I was thinking more about a poison with G.Doll, Nymph, and maybe Faros.

I tried Gramona and Karen in the event stories, but they didn't really click for me. Are they useful? Karen especially felt more like a filler slot. Maybe I'm just missing how they're supposed to be played?

Chaos 24 is good with 10 teams since she doesn’t take any good supports away and can still clear a5 with a little investment . Gdoll poison can be a separate team while chaos 24 can use characters who do almost nothing (you just need to double posse to scale your damage). Blue 24 has a problem of using lots of good characters to work

Yeah, I guess it should. I have tried it with other wheels, but it feels like I am just waiting for Arachne to charge up, while being defensless.
I had to use gnosis for each stage up to 1-10 of Arc 2.
Maybe I am not playing it correctly.

Thanks a lot, I'll try this 😁, and hopefully complete Arc 2.
I want to ask: does this team want to fuel up Vortice ? Since Saya's exalt is a bit expensive.

There are many teams that can beat story, it is more about learning to play them. Arachne is mostly a support, you must have a character with lifedrain and double keyflare wheels on non-saya teams + using dusk and dawn (WOD) on someone will really help. Jenkis relies on crit (and april praise is important), and you should be saving up enough swarm impacts to one shot combined with arachne exalt
I ended up doing that yesterday :) decided to stick with ghelot and that comp gave me a lot of space for mistakes.
Thanks all, I cleared madness first boss so far with gmurphy(OE), miryam (e0), murphy(e3) and a borrowed AA thais. Gonna try to see how far I can go.
I think a lot matters w the relic at the start of the run. It really helps.
Heres an update, Who should i build? Also im instrested in making an ultra and chaos team but im not which units i should get

If you want to go chaos there's not many choices to make : only the non-lilited standards are available for now.

In ultra, Pollux in fine but works best at E3 ( 4 copies ).
I wouldn't recommand putting Jenkins & Pollux kn the same team, so mqybe you can leave Pollux asides for now.

Be it Chaos or ultra, you're probably going to want some æquor / caro mixed in
Saya and Thaïs work well together but you need an efficient shield/healer
Tulu gives lots of tentacle damage so adding Faros or Celeste could go nicely
Helot:Catena dishes out lots of damage but need some preperation. Salvador, Faint or Aigis can sustain her in the meantime
There are other teams tou can try but basically just choose 1 or 2 awakeners you really like and start building from there
my team good for 3 days of playing?
Yes, it is ^^'
can i add something to it?
like a character?

Both Corpo and Helot:Catena work much better with their third enlightment ( 4 coies total ).
You will get access to more covenent and posse as you progress through the game.
About Wheels of Destiny, I recommand checking Cheri's guide

[AT LEAST 160 FREE STANDARD PULLS]

Go to the thread below for helpful Beginner guides and Redemption codes 

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1509396840330825758/1509396840330825758
You will eventually want 5 fonctionnal teams so pulling in all four realms fron standar could benefit you before chasing WoDs

And if you intend to invest money, pulling for a +12 WoD allows you to equip two wheels on the same awakener.

As for the rest ... it's probably better to ask when you actually hit a wall.
